channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Control and Alan Wake 2 To Get Upgraded PSSR Support
item.content:encoded:
Remedy Entertainment has announced that Control Ultimate Edition and Alan Wake 2 will support PS5 Pro’s upgraded PSSR.

The upgraded version of PlayStation 5 Pro’s PSSR was announced late last month, with Sony revealing that Resident Evil Requiem will be the first title to use the feature. More titles will start supporting the upgraded PSSR from March 2026, and now the next two games have been revealed.

Remedy Announces Upgraded PSSR Support for Control and Alan Wake 2

A new post on the official Remedy Entertainment X account (formerly Twitter) reveals that Control Ultimate Edition and Alan Wake 2 will receive support for the “advanced version of PSSR” soon. However, no date was revealed. Additionally, the post states that players will be able to look forward to “noticeable improvements in image stability clarity of motion.” Players will get to explore “the Oldest House and Bright Falls with a new level of visual fidelity.”

For those wondering about the upgraded PSSR, it is derived from Sony and AMD’s co-developed technology, Project Amethyst. It will provide the very latest from this technology, along with a new settings tool called  ‘Enhance PSSR Image Quality’. Through the new tool, players can check out the upgraded PSSR for any compatible game, with some titles bringing “noticeably crisper graphics.”

In other news, PlayStation Plus Game Catalog and Classics Catalog titles have been officially confirmed for March 2026. Sony is no longer releasing single-player PlayStation exclusives on PC. What are your thoughts on Control Ultimate Edition and Alan Wake 2 receiving support for PS5 Pro’s upgraded PSSR? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Cyberpunk 2077 devs having no plans to make any additional DLCs or expansions for the game. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Cyberpunk 2077 Devs Have ‘No Plans’ for Additional DLCs
item.content:encoded:
CD Projekt Red has confirmed that they currently have no intention of creating new DLCs or expansions for Cyberpunk 2077.

Cyberpunk 2077 is back in the news this month, thanks to its arrival on Xbox Game Pass Premium and Ultimate, allowing Series X/S and Xbox One players to check out CD Projekt Red’s hit title from 2020. This made players wonder whether more content was coming, but it doesn’t seem like that anymore.

Don’t Expect Any New Content for Cyberpunk 2077

In the announcement post for Cyberpunk 2077’s arrival on Xbox Game Pass, a fan asked the developers when the “secret new DLC” would arrive. The devs responded and shattered all the community’s hopes, stating that they have “no plans for additional DLCs or expansions. If anything changes, we will inform you all! So it seems like players will not get to try out any new Cyberpunk content for at least another four to five years, since the sequel is planned for then.

However, there might be another older game that CD Projekt Red might have new content planned for, and that’s none other than The Witcher 3. There have been multiple reports of a rumoured expansion for the game, seemingly arriving in May 2026 and taking players to a new region. But the developers haven’t officially confirmed any of these details, so players should take this information with a grain of salt.

In other news, Epic Games has teased another Cyberpunk 2077 x Fortnite collaboration on social media. Additionally, the developers have explained why the game works so well in first-person. What are your thoughts on Cyberpunk 2077 devs having ‘no plans’ for any additional DLCs or expansions? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Xbox Gaming Copilot coming to current-generation consoles this year. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Xbox Gaming Copilot Coming to Consoles This Year
item.content:encoded:
The popular AI assistant by Microsoft, Gaming Copilot, is coming to Xbox Series X/S in 2026.

Microsoft announced Xbox Copilot for gaming in March 2025 as a new tool that will become the “ultimate gaming sidekick” for players. Later in the year, it was announced that Xbox Insiders would have the opportunity to try this assistant on PCs and Windows handhelds. Now, it is on its way to consoles.

Xbox Series X/S Gaming Copilot Releases in 2026

According to a report by GamesRadar+, Sonali Yadav, Xbox’s gaming AI partner group product manager, announced the arrival of Gaming Copilot for current-gen Xbox consoles at GDC 2026. Yadav stated that it will arrive “later this year” and they will “continue to bring it to more services that players are playing.” Though the platforms weren’t confirmed, the company’s current-gen consoles are the Xbox Series X/S.

For those wondering about Xbox Gaming Copilot’s capabilities, it can currently provide players with gaming recommendations based on the games they are playing and their gaming history. The AI assistant will help players overcome obstacles and level up their gameplay. You can also ask Gaming Copilot for help, brainstorm strategies, and get tips or insights with personalized coaching. There’s also a voice conversation option available.

In other news, Microsoft is deleting all traces of the ‘This is an Xbox’ marketing campaign. Additionally, the Game Preservation team has teased the return of “iconic” games with “new ways to play”. What are your thoughts on Xbox Gaming Copilot coming to current-gen consoles? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Black Ops Royale failing to land well with the fans of the Warzone franchise. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android

item.title: New Escape from Tarkov Survey Focuses on Game’s Story Tasks
item.content:encoded:
Battlestate Games has published a new Escape from Tarkov survey designed around the game’s story missions. It prompts users to give their feedback on the campaign missions introduced in November with the 1.0 update, regardless of how far they’ve progressed through the story.

For participating in the survey, users can collect the usual assortment of rewards, including a Bulbex tool and GreenBat.

Did You Enjoy Tarkov’s Story Tasks?

Escape from Tarkov’s new survey asks questions about the average player’s progression through the game’s core campaign, introduced several months ago when the game hit 1.0 after years in a beta state.

In Battlestate’s words, it’s going to ‘help us determine the future direction of story tasks and improve your gameplay experience.’

The latter questions in the survey ask players about their desired outcome for completing more story quests. Do they want more rewards, mechanics, or interactions with traders, for instance?

There’s also a question around the frustrations raised by the story tasks. Are the actions too repetitive, is it too difficult to follow the objectives, or are the time restrictions too suffocating, for example?

When you complete the survey, you get a smattering of rewards:

  • 1 x Bulbex
  • 1 x Drain Cleaner
  • 1 x Fuel Conditioner
  • 1 x Green Battery

Escape from Tarkov’s story was a lauded addition to the game, giving it much more depth and allowing players to finally escape from Tarkov, completing the campaign and (hopefully) sailing off into the sunset – and an uncertain future.

Recently, Battlestate Games unveiled some endgame numbers, explaining how many players had managed to finish the story and adopt one of the game’s four endings.

It was confirmed that more than 32,000 people had completed Escape from Tarkov as of March 3.

Have you managed to complete Escape from Tarkov yet? Let us know what ending you got on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Warzone fans aren’t happy with Avalon

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: G2 and Liquid Swap MalbsMD and NertZ Ahead of Cologne Major
item.content:encoded:
Liquid and G2 have announced a swap deal, with Guy ‘NertZ’ Iluz and Mario ‘malbsMd’ Samayo switching jerseys in a move that sees Liquid return to the Americas region once again.

Question marks hang over malbsMD and NertZ as swap is confirmed

The swap sees the two players move after difficult periods in their respective teams, with both players having notable question marks over their individual levels of late.

After initially delivering in unfavourable anchor roles for G2, malbs had struggled to deliver with the increased responsibility of star positions since the departure of Nikola ‘NiKo’ Kovač.

The question then is whether malbs can deliver in star positions for Liquid. The Guatemalan shone in those same positions for M80, but struggled against Tier 1 opposition, making the move somewhat of a risk on Liquid’s part.

For NertZ, he is leaving a team that looked defeated and demotivated for much of its time together. However, arguments could be made that NertZ looked the least interested of the lot, regressing significantly in Liquid colours from his ENCE and Heroic form.

The Israeli rifler reunites with former ENCE and Heroic teammate, Álvaro ‘SunPayus’ García, as well as coach, Eetu ‘sAw’ Saha, recreating partnerships that saw NertZ play his best Counter-Strike, but recapturing that form is not necessarily a given.

Liquid returns to the North American region

So the swap deal has risks, but the move is safer from a different perspective, that being the VRS picture, where the move is set to make Liquid’s path to the IEM Cologne 2026 Major far easier.

The move would allow Liquid, who currently have two North American players, to swap to the Americas region for Major invite purposes once the Guatelaman is registered to the roster in the VRS.

The roster would move from 23rd in HLTV’s live EU VRS model to seventh in the Americas VRS, comfortably sitting within the 10 Americas invite spots for the Cologne Major.

Both Liquid and G2 are registered for local LANs, allowing the five matches needed to register players on the roster before the IEM Cologne 2026 Major invite cutoff.

Liquid are currently competing in the Roman Imperium Cup, with Justinas ‘⁠jL⁠’ Lekavicius standing in for the roster, accelerating the removal of NertZ from their official VRS roster.

How do you think malbsMD will do in Liquid? Discuss that, and more, on our Discord server!

For more CS news, see our report on NAVI’s victory over The MongolZ at ESL Pro League Season 23.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Is Everwind on Xbox Series X|S, PlayStation 5 or Nintendo Switch? All Platforms, Explained
item.content:encoded:
Releases continue to come thick and fast as gamers enter a busy release schedule, with Everwind among the slew of titles on the horizon, and platform details are a key point of discussion.

The RPG Sandbox Survival title launches into early access on March 17, giving players the first opportunity to enjoy a sky-faring adventure.

If Everwind has caught your eye and you’re curious about the platform details for the title, Insider Gaming has everything you need to know.

Can You Play Everwind on Xbox Series X|S?

No, Everwind is not playable on Xbox Series X|S for the early access launch, with the game exclusively available on Steam.

However, as the game is in early access and development is ongoing, a version for Xbox Series X|S and other platforms could be on the cards later down the line, closer to the full 1.0 launch.

At this moment in time, the focus is on continuing development on Everwind to add additional content for players to enjoy in early access, which will remain the priority for the foreseeable future.

Can You Play Everwind on PlayStation 5?

No, Everwind is not available on PlayStation 5 for the early access launch, which is instead exclusively available on PC via Steam.

Again, though, a console version on PlayStation 5 could be on the cards in the future, further in development, but it’s not a priority for the time being.

  • More Insider Gaming: Everwind Early Access Release Date & Exact Start Times

If you’re patient, however, Everwind could arrive on PlayStation 5 in the future, but there’s no concrete date for when such a release may occur.

Can You Play Everwind on Nintendo Switch 2?

No, Everwind is not available on Nintendo Switch 2. Instead, it’s exclusively available in early access on PC via Steam.

If Everwind is to have a console release further down the line, Nintendo Switch 2 would likely be further down the pecking order than the likes of Xbox Series X|S and PlayStation 5.

However, the considerable improvements to the system compared to its predecessor mean it is no longer going to be overlooked for big titles, and Everwind could, therefore, arrive later down the line.

What platforms do you want to see Everwind release on? Share your thoughts in the Insider Gaming Discord.

For more guides, check out all the details of the Pokémon TCG Chaos Rising set, and read about preload details for Crimson Desert.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Avalon Drops Into Warzone But Gets Negative Results From Fans
item.content:encoded:
It might not have hit your radar just yet, but Call of Duty: Warzone has a new map. It seems to have come without much fuss, removing Verdansk from the game and replacing it with this endgame map pulled from Black Ops 7, and in the first few hours of it landing, it has produced a mixed reception (but mostly negative) from fans.

It’s called Avalon, and it’s an evolution over Blackout, the ‘original’ large-scale battle royale map launched with Black Ops 4 in 2018. The premise is great, but it has received a bit of blowback because it has awkward pacing and fewer players than some would like.

Avalon Doesn’t Land With Warzone Players

Firstly, it’s important to stress that all other modes in Warzone have been removed to make room for ‘Black Ops Royale’, which is the playlist name for Avalon. That’s strike one for the players who couldn’t care less about the new map.

In one comment on social media, a user noted that it felt like ‘REDSEC and Fortnite’ combined. There are no loadouts, and players must rely on what they can find to dominate the battlefield, which might be strike two.

Also, the player count has been slashed to just 100 users, but the map remains a fairly chunky size. That’s strike three.

One user had a damning statement about the mode:

Disappointment is an understatement, I was looking forward to this for a long time. It’s rough. Just got off after playing for over two hours and just uninstalled and decided to move on for good.

In terms of the rotations on Avalon, only quads can be played at present, which, believe it or not, is strike four.

There are a few positive comments to draw from regarding Avalon, though.

In one comment, someone wrote that the map is great, but the mode is not, while another said it’s fun but requested a solo queue for the mode.

The arrival of Avalon also didn’t herald much of a boost for the game in terms of viewership and players. On Twitch, the game went from peaks of around 33,000 to just 54,000 viewers, and on Steam (one of the only platforms for public numbers), the player count for Call of Duty in general saw no tangible increase compared to recent days.

Have you dropped into Avalon yet? Let us know what you think about the map on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that The Simpsons: Hit & Run revival isn’t far-fetched

Written by

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant's adoration for gaming started on the Amstrad CPC in 1996. Now, he's a multi-platform expert gamer with a deep love for the industry, a passion for esports, and more…

More from Grant Taylor-Hill
